strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance in long-horizon agent memory
Well-defined method: claim schema, linker, and truth-consistent retrieval
Strong empirical design: controlled baselines with same reader/embedding
Ablation study isolates contribution of contradict/supersede edges
Efficiency analysis shows reduced token cost vs. baseline
weaknesses: Results rely heavily on placeholder macros; actual performance unknown
Temporal-reasoning results (TR) are weak and sample size is tiny (N=5)
No statistical significance testing or error bars reported
Limited discussion of failure modes or edge cases of the linker
Comparison to SOTA memory systems is limited to A-Mem and flat RAG
required_revisions: Replace all placeholder result macros (e.g., \TTMGLMEAcc, \FlatLMEAcc) with actual numeric results from experiments.
Clarify the sample size and variance for temporal-reasoning (TR) slice; do not hide weak results in text.
Add statistical significance tests (e.g., confidence intervals, p-values) for accuracy differences.
Include a detailed error analysis beyond the single qualitative example.
Provide full results on LoCoMo and MemoryAgentBench CR split, not just LongMemEval-S.
Justify hyperparameter choices (e.g., similarity gate 0.65, k=8) with ablation or citations.
Discuss limitations: e.g., linker errors, scalability of pairwise comparisons, handling of implicit contradictions.

strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance for long-horizon agents

Strong ablation isolating the linker's contribution
Efficiency analysis shows lower compute than baseline
weaknesses: Results rely on placeholder macros; no raw data or significance tests
Temporal-reasoning slice (N=5) too small for reliable conclusions
No statistical significance reported for claimed gains
Missing details on random seed variance and standard deviations
Cross-domain evaluation on LoCoMo/MemoryAgentBench not reported

strengths: Novel integration of truth maintenance with graph-based memory for agents
Clear focus on practical problem: maintaining factual consistency over time
Efficiency claims via session-batched writing and gated linking
weaknesses: Results rely heavily on placeholder macros; actual performance unverified
Temporal-reasoning results (40%) are weak and sample size tiny (N=5)
Token and latency improvements are relative to a reimplementation, not original A-Mem
No discussion of system integration complexity or failure modes
Scalability of pairwise linking for large claim graphs is unclear
